channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Biden admin to provide $750 million to North Carolina-based Wolfspeed for advanced computer chips
item.description: The Biden-Harris administration announced plans to provide up to $750 million in direct funding to Wolfspeed, with the money supporting its new silicon carbide factory in North Carolina that makes the wafers used in advanced computer chips and its factory in Marcy, New York.
item.dc:creator: Josh Boak, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Innovación y mejora continua en la granja: la apuesta por la excelencia operativa de Costa Food Group
item.description: El Grupo Empresarial Costa vio la luz en 1966 en la localidad oscense de Fraga. Desde entonces, su crecimiento y expansión han sido envidiables, llegando a traspasar fronteras y afincándose en más de un centenar de países.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Google to buy nuclear power for AI’s energy needs
item.description: Alphabet’s Google said on Monday it signed the world’s first corporate agreement to buy power from multiple small modular reactors as the technology company looks to meet electricity demand from artificial intelligence.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, Fast Company

item.title: What in the world is going on at Ubisoft?
item.description: Ubisoft is no stranger to takeover whispers. In 2004, Electronic Arts made a run at the video game publisher, but was ultimately rebuffed. In 2015, Vivendi made an even more serious attempt that took years to defuse.
item.dc:creator: Chris Morris, Fast Company

item.title: Adobe lets customers test Firefly AI video generator
item.description: Adobe’s AI model for video generation is now available in a limited beta, enabling users to create short video clips from text and image prompts.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Bankinter explora vías de aumento de la eficiencia a través de la IA
item.description: Bankinter, de la mano de Microsoft, está implantando desde hace aproximadamente un año Microsoft 365 Copilot en diversas áreas del banco.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Windows users are being tricked by sneaky malware scheme
item.description: Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson reveals how security experts found new malware, Lumma Stealer, spreading via fake verification pages.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Murder trial of Nima Momeni in death of Cash App founder Bob Lee begins
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — The murder trial of a tech consultant in the stabbing death of Cash App founder Bob Lee begins Monday, a year and a half after the widely admired entrepreneur was found staggering on a deserted downtown San Francisco street seeking help.
item.dc:creator: JANIE HAR, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Volunteers bring solar power to Hurricane Helene's disaster zone
item.description: BAKERSVILLE, N.C. (AP) — Nearly two weeks after Hurricane Helene downed power lines and washed out roads all over North Carolina's mountains, the constant din of a gas-powered generator is getting to be too much for Bobby Renfro.
item.dc:creator: GABRIELA AOUN ANGUEIRA, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: How to use Passwords, Apple iOS 18’s new iPhone password management app
item.description: Apple iOS 18, launched in September, is one of the most important updates to the iPhone’s operating system in the device’s history. Why? Because it lays the groundwork for Apple Intelligence, Apple’s artificial intelligence platform, which will power the phone in the years ahead.
item.dc:creator: Michael Grothaus, Fast Company

item.title: Google monopoly: Prosecutors hint at company breakup, but analysts say it’s unlikely
item.description: The case was filed in 2020, heralding a new U.S. government legal effort to rein in tech giants, with anti-monopoly cases against Amazon, Apple, and Facebook and Instagram parent firm Meta.
item.dc:creator: Ethan Baron, The Mercury News

item.title: Opinion: Musk’s courtship with MAGA has driven X into a misinformation abyss
item.description: If X is a town square, it's inundated with an uncontrolled, right-wing mob. And Musk is their cheering benefactor.
item.dc:creator: Lorraine Ali, The Mercury News

item.title: Emergency SOS Live Video: A Game-Changer for iPhone Users
item.description: Apple's new Emergency SOS Live Video feature allows iPhone users to share live video footage with first responders, enhancing emergency response and safety.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: AMD boosts AI performance in new line of enterprise PC chips
item.description: AMD’s big Advancing AI event in San Francisco on Thursday underlined how quickly the microprocessor industry has pivoted to artificial intelligence (AI) as its main sales pitch.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Key supplier of this critical semiconductor material is back in operation post-Helene
item.description: One of the two companies that manufacture high-purity quartz used for making semiconductors and other high-tech products from mines in a western North Carolina community severely damaged by Hurricane Helene is operating again.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: Microsoft’s Patch Tuesday updates: Keeping up with the latest fixes
item.description: Long before Taco Tuesday became part of the pop-culture vernacular, Tuesdays were synonymous with security — and for anyone in the tech world, they still are.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: The IRS is rolling out its free tax-filing tool to 30 million Americans—and surprisingly, it’s great
item.description: Some 30 million Americans across the country will have a new way to file their taxes next spring: Direct File, a tool developed by the Internal Revenue Service (IRS) that allows users to prepare their taxes and file online completely free of charge and without the need for any third-party provider.
item.dc:creator: Bryce Covert, Fast Company

item.title: Hurricane Milton Sparks Concern Over Spontaneous Combustion of Electrical Vehicles
item.description: Florida and other hurricane zones face a growing concern - the spontaneous combustion of electrical vehicles flooded by salty storm surges
item.dc:creator: Tribune News Service, The Mercury News

item.title: Army Corps of Engineers modernizes IT infrastructure through hybrid cloud Vertosoft
item.description: The Army Corps of Engineers will complete its hybrid cloud implementation by the end of fiscal 2025. Dovarius Peoples, former Corps’ CIO, lays out where the org The post Army Corps of Engineers modernizes IT infrastructure through hybrid cloud Vertosoft first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Anastasia Obis, Federal News Network

item.title: MoneyGram hack exposes customers' sensitive data, cripples services
item.description: MoneyGram, an American payment and money transfer platform, confirmed a data breach that exposed sensitive customer data to hackers, forcing its services offline for five days.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Pentagon releases final CMMC rule, paving way for implementation
item.description: The Pentagon has taken another big step forward to make CMMC a reality. DoD projects the requirements will start showing up in contracts by mid-2025. The post Pentagon releases final CMMC rule, paving way for implementation first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Justin Doubleday, Federal News Network

item.title: A whopping 342,817 Lego Technic pieces are used to build a sports car
item.description: Lego and McLaren, the British supercar manufacturer, teamed up to create a fully drivable, life-sized replica of the McLaren P1 hypercar.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: The Toxic Cloud Triad: A Growing Security Concern - 38% of Organizations at High Risk
item.description: A recent study reveals that 38% of organizations have at least one cloud workload that is critically vulnerable, highly privileged, and publicly exposed, creating a high-risk attack path for bad actors.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: North Carolina maker of high-purity quartz back operating post-Helene
item.description: SPRUCE PINE, N.C. (AP) — One of the two companies that manufacture high-purity quartz used for making semiconductors and other high-tech products from mines in a western North Carolina community severely damaged by Hurricane Helene is operating again.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Cisco revamps key DevNet sandboxes
item.description: Cisco has expanded its DevNet sandbox environments to make it easier for network professionals to test and develop applications for key enterprise networking technologies.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Cloudera jockeys for AI platform prominence
item.description: While the big hyperscalers are fighting it out for dominance in the AI space, many enterprises are looking for alternatives. More than half (60%) of large companies have pulled some AI workloads from public clouds, according to a survey released this summer by Flexential.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Scammers exploit grief with fake funeral streaming on Facebook
item.description: Scammers are offering video streaming for funeral services and taking advantage of people who are grieving to get credit card information.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Five days a week in the office? Forget it!
item.description: A few weeks ago, Amazon CEO Andy Jassy announced that all corporate employees, including AWS staffers, will be expected to work in the office five days a week beginning in January. I don’t work for Amazon, but if I did, I’d quit. Period. End of statement.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Microsoft previews AI ‘building blocks’ for .NET
item.description: Microsoft is previewing packages geared to integrating generative AI services into .NET applications. Called Microsoft.Extensions.AI.Abstractions and Microsoft.Extensions.AI libraries, the packages are available in preview as of October 8.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Effective EDR: Balancing testing rigor and velocity
item.description: Various industries are still dealing with the consequences of the July 2024 technology outage which led to a "blue screen of death" and extreme disruptions. The post Effective EDR: Balancing testing rigor and velocity first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: commentators, Federal News Network

item.title: How to use your iPhone to get help by satellite
item.description: Apple’s decision to put satellite connections in iPhones is already saving lives, and as Hurricane Milton threatens Florida, now is the time for every US iPhone user to learn how the system works and why they should upgrade to iOS 18.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Sanders: Mark Cuban wrong to call for Lina Khan to be replaced
item.description: Sen. Bernie Sanders (I-Vt.) publicly disagreed with Mark Cuban after he called for Federal Trade Commissioner (FTC) Chair Lina Khan to be replaced if Vice President Harris is elected president. In a post on the social platform X, Sanders said Cuban “is wrong” when he called for a new FTC chair.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, The Hill

item.title: American Water cyberattack renews focus on protecting critical infrastructure
item.description: CAMDEN, N.J. (AP) — A cyberattack continues to affect the largest regulated water and wastewater utility company in the United States, renewing a focus on the importance of protecting critical infrastructure sites.
item.dc:creator: BRUCE SHIPKOWSKI, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: US considers asking court to break up Google as it weighs remedies in the antitrust case
item.description: The U.S. Department of Justice is considering asking a federal judge to break up Google after its ubiquitous search engine was declared an illegal monopoly, but it is just one of many possible remedies under review, according to a court filing.
item.dc:creator: SHAWN CHEN, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Google loses final EU court appeal against 2.4 billion euro fine in antitrust shopping case
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Google lost its final legal challenge on Tuesday against a European Union penalty for giving its own shopping recommendations an illegal advantage over rivals in search results, ending a long-running antitrust case that came with a whopping fine.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Someone reinvented the toilet so that you can do your business anywhere
item.description: Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson reveals how the Generation 2 Reinvented Toilet transforms waste into safe materials, a promising sanitation solution that could transform lives and communities.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Electron vs. Tauri: Which cross-platform framework is for you?
item.description: If there’s one thing developers agree on about cross-platform applications, it’s that it’s easier to create a GUI with HTML, CSS, and JavaScript than to use one of the many cross-platform frameworks out there. Frameworks like Electron give you the best of both worlds.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: TikTok is designed to be addictive to kids and causes them harm, US states' lawsuits say
item.description: More than a dozen states and the District of Columbia filed lawsuits against TikTok on Tuesday, saying that the popular short-form video app is designed to be addictive to kids and harms their mental health.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO and BARBARA ORTUTAY, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: IoT-driven endpoint proliferation requires secure-by-design principles
item.description: The government needs to push the market in the direction of secure-by-design, including manufacturing, fielding and management of these new endpoints. The post IoT-driven endpoint proliferation requires secure-by-design principles first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Daisy Thornton, Federal News Network

item.title: Aryaka brings CASB into unified SASE fold
item.description: Aryaka continues to build out its SASE platform, adding new capabilities aimed at enhancing security and simplifying the adoption process for enterprises that want to consolidate their network and security deployments. At the heart of this update is Aryaka’s new cloud access security broker (CASB).
item.dc:creator: Network World
